examples.title: CYK Algorithm
examples.description: Parsing algorithm that requires grammar in CNF
examples.code:
FUNCTION CYK(w, grammar):
 n = length(w)
 // Create table for substrings
 CREATE table T[n][n]
 
 // Initialize diagonal with terminals
 FOR i = 0 to n-1:
 FOR each production A → w[i]:
 T[i][i].add(A)
 
 // Fill table for longer substrings
 FOR len = 2 to n:
 FOR i = 0 to n-len:
 j = i + len - 1
 FOR k = i to j-1:
 FOR each production A → BC:
 IF B in T[i][k] AND C in T[k+1][j]:
 T[i][j].add(A)
 
 // Check if start symbol derives full string
 RETURN S in T[0][n-1]
examples.explanation: The CYK algorithm parses a string by dynamic programming. It builds a table where T[i][j] contains all non-terminals that can derive the substring from position i to j. Requires grammar in CNF form.

examples.title: Remove ε-Productions
examples.description: Algorithm to eliminate nullable productions from CFG
examples.code:
FUNCTION RemoveEpsilon(grammar):
 // Find nullable non-terminals
 nullable = {}
 REPEAT:
 changed = false
 FOR each production A → α:
 IF α contains only terminals: continue
 IF all symbols in α are in nullable:
 IF A not in nullable:
 nullable.add(A)
 changed = true
 UNTIL NOT changed
 
 // Generate new productions
 newProductions = {}
 FOR each production A → α:
 FOR each subset of nullable positions in α:
 β = α with symbols at positions removed
 IF β is not empty:
 newProductions.add(A → β)
 
 // Remove ε-productions from original
 RETURN grammar without ε-productions ∪ newProductions
examples.explanation: This algorithm finds all nullable non-terminals (those that can derive ε) and then generates new productions by removing nullable symbols in all possible combinations.

examples.title: Remove Unit Productions
examples.code:
FUNCTION RemoveUnit(grammar):
 // Compute unit closure for each non-terminal
 FOR each non-terminal A:
 unitClosure[A] = {A}
 REPEAT:
 changed = false
 FOR each unit production B → C:
 IF B in unitClosure[A]:
 IF C not in unitClosure[A]:
 unitClosure[A].add(C)
 changed = true
 UNTIL NOT changed
 
 // Generate new non-unit productions
 newProductions = {}
 FOR each non-terminal A:
 FOR each B in unitClosure[A]:
 FOR each production B → α (non-unit):
 newProductions.add(A → α)
 
 RETURN newProductions
examples.explanation: This computes the transitive closure of unit productions, then replaces each unit production A→B with all non-unit productions that B can derive.

examples.title: Convert to Binary Form
examples.description: Split productions with more than 2 non-terminals
examples.code:
FUNCTION ToBinaryForm(grammar):
 newProductions = {}
 counter = 0
 
 FOR each production A → α:
 IF length(α) ≤ 2:
 newProductions.add(A → α)
 ELSE:
 // Split: A → X₁ X₂ ... Xₖ where k > 2
 // Create k-2 new variables
 symbols = list(α)
 current = symbols[0]
 
 FOR i = 1 to length(symbols)-2:
 newVar = X_{counter}
 counter = counter + 1
 newProductions.add(current → symbols[i] newVar)
 current = newVar
 
 // Add final production
 newProductions.add(current → symbols[length(symbols)-2] symbols[length(symbols)-1])
 
 RETURN newProductions
examples.explanation: This algorithm introduces new non-terminals to break down productions with more than 2 symbols on the RHS into a chain of binary productions, as required by CNF.
